title: [create-instantsearch-app] Allow more flexible dependencies
body:
The way we fetch the correct library version for each template right now is defined by the [`libraryName` property on each template configuration](https://github.com/algolia/create-instantsearch-app/blob/6e674b707223b5bf5097205e17b7e52cefbfcfda/templates/InstantSearch.js/.template.js#L5). Then, we [fetch the library versions](https://github.com/algolia/create-instantsearch-app/blob/6e674b707223b5bf5097205e17b7e52cefbfcfda/packages/cli/cli.js#L127) based on this name.

This is fine for now but won't work as soon as we release new major versions (i.e. InstantSearch 3) because we will rely on the libraries `instantsearch.js` and `algoliasearch`.

Here is my proposal.

## Template configuration file

What I propose is to remove this `libraryName` property and to add a `dependencies` property in the `.template.js` file:

```javascript
module.exports = {
  dependencies: [
    {
      title: 'InstantSearch.js',
      name: 'instantsearch.js',
    },
    {
      title: 'JavaScript Client',
      name: 'algoliasearch',
    },
  ]
};
```

## CLI

Then, in the CLI, we prompt all these dependencies (instead of the current "InstantSearch version"). It would look like so:

```
InstantSearch.js version: 2.8.0
JavaScript Client version: 2.5.0
```

We can then add these versions in a `versions` key in the config passed to `createInstantSearchApp()`.

## Templates

Using Handlebars, we'll be able to access them in the template:

```hbs
{{versions.[instantsearch.js]}}
{{versions.[algoliasearch]}}
```

I think this solution is more future proof; I already needed such a feature for installing templates without dependencies, and other templates depending on CSS libraries to fetch on npm.

---

Let me know what you think.
